cases.title: One digest, two public readers
cases.summary: A deterministic projection is generated once, consumed by human pages and JSON clients, and deployed as an immutable Pages release.
cases.label: engineering case study
cases.learned: Binding both readers to one digest turns disagreement into measurable drift.
cases.limits: This is an implementation result, not evidence that the knowledge methodology produces true findings.

cases.title: Telemetry failure stays off the hot path
cases.summary: Primary telemetry append failure falls back to an ownership-checked spool while research execution continues.
cases.label: engineering case study
cases.learned: A receipt can preserve delivery degradation without turning optional observability into a system-wide stop condition.
cases.limits: Tests establish software behavior; they do not establish an empirical law about all telemetry systems.

cases.title: The build refuses unsupported findings
cases.summary: Canon validation and publication guards run before every site build, rejecting results without a valid Decision-to-Evidence chain.
cases.label: engineering case study
cases.learned: Moving the epistemic rule into the build makes unsupported publication unavailable rather than merely discouraged.
cases.limits: The guard proves contract enforcement, not the truth of any future Evidence.

cases.title: A failed review gate remains visible
cases.summary: The system preserved a launcher failure, repaired the execution boundary, then stopped when the real no-cache gate failed 14 of 14 cases.
cases.label: engineering case study
cases.learned: Separating launcher failure from model evaluation prevents infrastructure noise from becoming a scientific verdict.
cases.limits: This retrospective receipt is not a prospective denominator and produced zero Evidence.
